pr.title: Create `revert_asm.py` to unlink C files from `make`
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Added a new Python helper, `tools/revert_asm.py`, that takes a source C file path, disassembles its built object back into an asm `.s` file using `build/tools/dtk elf disasm`, and edits `obj_files.mk` so `make` uses the generated asm file instead of the C source. The PR is a small tooling change for quickly reverting a decompiled C translation unit back to assembly in the build graph.

key_files.role: New command-line tool for reverting a `src/.../*.c` file to an `asm/.../*.s` input in `obj_files.mk`.

what_changed: Created executable script `tools/revert_asm.py` with an argparse CLI: `usage: revert_asm.py [-h] C_FILE`.

The script resolves the provided C file relative to the repository `src` directory, computes the matching object path under `build/GALE01/obj`, and computes the corresponding asm path under `asm` with a `.s` suffix.
It invokes `build/tools/dtk elf disasm <obj_path> <asm_path>` via `subprocess.run(..., check=True)` to produce assembly from the built object.
It handles Windows by appending `.exe` to the `build/tools/dtk` path when `platform.system() == "Windows"`.
After disassembly, it rewrites `obj_files.mk`, replacing the source path such as `src/.../*.c` with the asm path such as `asm/.../*.s`.
smart_moves: Uses `Path(__file__).parents[1]` to locate the repository root from the tool location instead of depending on the caller's working directory.
Uses `path.resolve().relative_to(root / "src")` so the input must correspond to a file inside `src`, then derives object and asm paths consistently from that relative path.
Uses `Path.with_suffix()` to convert both C/source-relative and object paths to `.s` and `.o`, avoiding fragile string suffix manipulation.
Runs `dtk` with `check=True`, so failures in disassembly stop the script instead of silently editing `obj_files.mk` after a failed conversion.
Normalizes paths with `.as_posix()` before replacing entries in `obj_files.mk`, matching typical makefile path formatting and helping cross-platform use.
decomp_lessons: A small reversible tooling path is useful when a C file is not ready to stay linked into the build: disassemble the current object and switch the makefile entry back to asm.
For decomp workflows, derive `src`, `asm`, and `build/.../obj` paths from one source-relative path to reduce mistakes when moving a translation unit between C and assembly.
When mutating build lists, make the change narrow and mechanical: this script performs a direct `obj_files.mk` text replacement from `src/...` to `asm/...`.
Cross-platform decomp tooling should account for Windows executable suffixes even for internal build tools such as `dtk`.
naming_conventions: Tool name uses an action-oriented snake_case filename: `revert_asm.py`.
CLI metavar is uppercase and descriptive: `C_FILE`.
Internal path variables follow explicit suffix naming: `obj_path`, `src_path`, `asm_path`, `dtk_path`, and `mk_path`.
The script describes its purpose as `revert a C file to asm for make`, matching the PR title's wording about unlinking C files from `make`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Uses `dtk elf disasm` to regenerate an asm `.s` file from the built `.o` corresponding to a C source file.
Maps `src/<relative>.c` to `asm/<relative>.s` and `build/GALE01/obj/<relative>.o`, preserving the source-relative directory structure.
Switches the active build input by replacing the `src` path with the `asm` path in `obj_files.mk`, rather than deleting source files or manually editing broader build configuration.
follow_up_queries: Find later changes to `tools/revert_asm.py` for robustness improvements or bug fixes.
Search PRs that edit `obj_files.mk` to switch files between `src` and `asm`.
Search for tooling that invokes `dtk elf disasm` or other `dtk` subcommands.
Check whether later build tooling replaced direct `obj_files.mk` text replacement with a generated source list workflow.
